key_insight: GMHF uses expert feedback to steer a generative meta-learner toward the unseen target distribution, improving generalization under distribution shift.
engineering_relevance: For materials discovery and other high-stakes engineering settings with scarce target data, this offers a practical human-in-the-loop route to more robust surrogate models and decision support.
method: The framework combines a Conditional Neural ODE (cNODE) as a generative digital twin with a reinforcement learning agent.
The agent iteratively adjusts latent physical parameters using human feedback to better match target-physics beliefs.
Theory provides generalization-error bounds showing that reducing divergence between generated and target data lowers risk.
evidence_signal: In experiments on a nonlinear Duffing oscillator, deployment loss decreased as expert reliability increased, and generated-vs-target divergence also fell; a probabilistic non-dynamical test confirmed broader applicability.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and consistency of expert feedback, so unreliable or biased human inputs could misdirect the generator.
paper_title: Human-Machine Collaboration on Generative Meta-Learning: Model and Algorithm
